sites.desc_no: Vannkraftrik kystlokasjon, 12,5 MW-moduler skalerer til 50 MW+ campus.

sites.name: GIGA-42 Bakki
sites.desc_en: Industrial greenfield site with geothermal baseload, 50 MW initial deployment in 2027, scaling up to 150MW+ by 2030
sites.desc_no: Geotermisk grunnlast, 50 MW oppstart 2027, skalerer til 200 MW.
sites.category_rationale: Develop / Dispose — develop if land and power can be secured near term; otherwise sell if a material offer is obtained from Meralyte or others.
sites.nbs_assessment: Support from PM's office and municipality. Land secured by MOU; lease under negotiation. Subsea cables being renewed with new connections to North America. Draft PPA terms received but utility/TSO want end-user interest before definitive contracts.
sites.project_description: Greenfield plot adjoining the (now ceased) PCC Silicon plant. Mostly flat, no trees, small stream to one side. Old pre-fabricated office buildings on part of the site. Substation across the road on the site boundary.
sites.exact_power_status: Under LOI with utility. Power & transmission available subject to PPA.
sites.fibre_strands: Dark fibre available (via FARICE extension); dedicated strands possible under future subsea scenario; allocation TBC.
sites.fibre_general_info: One of the strongest-connected sites in the Nordics. Three independent subsea systems land in Iceland (FARICE, DANICE, IRIS) with direct routes to UK, mainland Europe and North America. Systems being renewed and expanded with new/upgraded NA connectivity. Multiple technical and commercial discussions with FARICE via JLL.
sites.latency_summary: Dublin ~13.4 ms · Slough UK ~15.5–19.9 ms · Frankfurt ~19.9 ms · Amsterdam ~19.7 ms · Oslo ~22.2 ms · Paris ~22.7 ms · NY ~42.8 ms (IRIS).
sites.zoning_status: Parcel A zoned (one plot complete); Parcel B (expansion) zoning to be conducted by the municipality (~12 months estimated).
sites.key_risks_dependencies: Power procurement — Landsvirkjun's new process requires an end user prior to MOU signing (currently mitigated by an LOI from Solstice Data).
sites.current_control: Lease negotiations (extension to MOU agreed Feb-26)
sites.planned_ultimate_control: Effective freehold (long leasehold; freehold not possible in Iceland)

sites.area_overview: Húsavík is a town in Norðurþing municipality on the northeast coast of Iceland on the shores of Skjálfandi bay with 2,485 inhabitants. The most famous landmark of the town is the wooden church Húsavíkurkirkja, built in 1907. Húsavík is served by Húsavík Airport.

sites.name: GIGA-42 Skibotn
sites.desc_en: Sub-Arctic site and candidate for the Norwegian landing point of the Far North Fibre Project.
sites.desc_no: Sub-arktisk norsk lokasjon med vannkraftkapasitet og frikjøling på 69° N.
sites.category_rationale: Develop / Mining or Dispose — shovel-ready in medium term (Q1-27) subject to power. Only 15 MW so possibly more suited to mining; alternatively landowner not opposed to bitcoin mining.
sites.nbs_assessment: Secure but small. Close to new Arctic fibre project linking North Norway to North America/Canada/Japan. Municipality investigating concession power (2-3 MW est., not yet included). No employment tax in municipality.
sites.project_description: Identified by the Scale42 team in 2023 as a strong data-centre candidate at the Norwegian landing point of the Far North Fibre project. Backed by both the current landowner and the municipality. Brownfield site with existing industrial buildings; surrounding fjord sparsely populated with natural woodland. Greenfield development possible on areas within the plot.
sites.exact_power_status: In queue, awaiting allocation. 1st in local DSO queue, 2nd in regional TSO queue.
sites.heat_reuse: Municipal support for study engaging local businesses.
sites.fibre_general_info: Carrier-diverse terrestrial fibre via the northern Norwegian backbone, with southbound routing into Sweden and continental Europe.
sites.latency_summary: Oslo ~10–15 ms · Stockholm ~15–20 ms · Frankfurt ~25–30 ms · London ~30–35 ms. Subsea: indirect via Sweden/Denmark (DANICE, IRIS).
sites.other_access_notes: Close proximity to Far North Fibre project.
sites.zoning_status: Plot zoned but not for data centre. Strong municipal political support; special dispensation granted (expires Q3 2028). Detailed zoning expected within 12 months.
sites.key_risks_dependencies: Mayor and landowner need consistent direction on local engagement.
sites.contractual_arrangement: Roald Normann Consulting (land agent). Ground works included within land purchase.

ProjectID: NOR003
Primary Project Partner: GIGA-42
Potential FID Date: Allocation of power by Statnett - Estimated by June 2026 Obtain Building Permit - September 2026 FID Phase I (7MW) = September 2026
Potential RFS Dates: Assuming 12 month construction commencing upon FID RFS Phase 1 = September 2027
Project Lead: Daniel Gussias
Agent: No agent
Plot Name: Gnr: 45 Bnr: 179, Storfjord Municipality
Plot Priority: 1
Site Address: Nordlysveien 3465, 9143 Skibotn, Norway
Location Link: https://maps.app.goo.gl/GRzBahhr5VMY3uLg6
Not Required: 69.337176, 20.350427
Maps & Drawings: Insert Link
Photos & Drone Footage: Insert Link
Size (ha) - Primary Plot: 3
Description: Located within the centre of the Skibotn Fjord, this is a brownfield site with exisiting industrial buildings, surrounded by natural woodland.
Site Drainage: The site is adjacent to the E8 highway that connects northern Finland to Norway, located ~6km from the town of Skitbotn. Exisiting brownfield infrastructure sees this site having suitable access at two points from the main road. However the same access road is used for a holiday park, so additional roads/ security measures would need to be taken to ensure site security. Current brownfield infrastructure is sufficient for drainage concerns.
Site Access: The site is adjacent to the E8 highway that connects northern Finland to Norway, located ~6km from the town of Skitbotn. Exisiting brownfield infrastructure sees this site having suitable access at two points from the main road. However the same access road is used for a holiday park, so additional roads/ security measures would need to be taken to ensure site security. Current brownfield infrastructure is sufficient for drainage concerns.
Building Rights (%) - Primary Plot: % will be set under detailregulation to industry
Ownership: SKH EIENDOM & INVEST AS (Company)
Zoning Status: Plot is not currently zoned for industry, but dispensation is confirmed in writing. This allows us up to 3 years to zone the plot and can be built upon and used as a datacentre during that time.
Heat Off-Take: Potential to bring in heat offtakers local to the DC site, or provide heated storage facilities.
Fibre Connectivity: Far North Arcitic Fibre Project is landing in Skibottn as the first connection in Europe. https://www.farnorthfiber.com/ "Expecting dark dibre availability and 2+ carriers available at most locations identified in Norway. Otherwise Stelia have confirmed across all Norway sites that sufficient dark fibre connectivity for AI data centres can be implemented. James is continuing investigations directly with local fibre providers to obtain more detailed information."
Power Connection Methods: A 7 MW power draw is possible to be implemented in the regional grid without addiitonal measures. A new transformer will be installed to accommodate the remaining power draw above 7MW. This assumes a number of investments in the distribution network, such as the establishment of a new feeder from the existing Skibotn substation – dedicated to our consumption. A suitable customer interface will be established at our connection point. The network infrastructure we develop within the distribution grid will be considered a customer-specific installation.
Power Connection Timelines: Needs a new main transformer at the local substation. New transformer/gas-insulated switchgear/component timeline is likely 2 years and timeline for concession is likely to be approximately 1 year. Therefore expecting a 3 year timeline but seeking to shorten this through discussions with NVE who give approvals for the concessions to speed up the concession process. Also connecting the DSO with suppliers we know who can supply transformers more quickly. Where those suppliers are not acceptable to the DSO we will engage domestic electrical engineering firms about recertifying and warranting transformers from alternative suppliers to the DSO.
Power Procurement Status: Submitted 2x applications splitting capacity to the local grid company Passed the maturity test process Been placed in Statnet queue First in queue for local susbtation Awaiting to reach front of queue for main transmission substation
Power Availability Confirmation: Yes
Acquisition Strategy: We have secured an option agreement for the land which expires on 26th August 2027 At any time during the option we can commence a lease of the site which will include options for us to purchase the site during that lease.
Zoning Strategy: DISP inplace (with full political support). Zoning will take about 1 year from when the process starts. Zoning needs to be complete before new election (2027)
Building Permit Strategy: A technical consultant such as Ramboll can be instructed to prepare and submit the building permits. Including technical design this is forecast to cost circa 60k EUR. The strategy would be to submit short form building permits which can be prepared, submitted and approved in 2-4 months.
Power Procurement Strategy: Once a short-form building permit is approved, an application can be made to Fingrid including ramp up plans and connection details. This would then be instantly approved and capacity allocated for a new 400kV line. The existing line 110kV line would not require a connection application.
Power Provider: Arva AS
Power Connection Costs: 2.3mil EUR per 400kV connection plus cost of construction. No cost expected to use the existing 110kV connection.
Fibre Provider: Global Connect likely best positioned to provide
Price / sqm: 1600 NOK per sqm
Lease Rate (per annum): 300 NOK per sqm
Purchase Price: 45 636 000 NOK (Dont have to buy whole plot)
Fees: None
Next Steps: Agree price for site with Stora Enso.

sites.name: GIGA-42 Reykjavík
sites.desc_en: Greater Reykjavík capacity adjacent to existing fibre — low-latency to North America.
sites.desc_no: Kapasitet i Stor-Reykjavík ved siden av eksisterende fiber — lav latency til Nord-Amerika.
sites.category_rationale: Develop / Dispose.
sites.nbs_assessment: GIGA-42 lead for powered land supplied by Reykjavík Energy (RE) in Municipality of Ölfus. Commercial discussions with RE underway; GIGA-42 well positioned.
sites.project_description: Early discussion with ON Power (Reykjavík Energy subsidiary) to acquire land and power near Reykjavík. 70,000 sqm available, fully zoned for DC; additional 210,000 sqm available. Power and land both owned by ON Power. Geothermal direct from Hellisheiði — no grid connection agreement required. Heat-reuse opportunities within the geothermal park.
sites.exact_power_status: In negotiations with utility. Power & transmission understood available subject to PPA.
sites.heat_reuse: Industrial park has existing tenants that could take heat (e.g. direct-air carbon capture).
sites.fibre_general_info: Fastest UK connection within Iceland; closest DC to FARICE cable.
sites.latency_summary: London ~15–20 ms · Amsterdam ~18–25 ms · Frankfurt ~20–28 ms · Copenhagen ~20–30 ms · NY ~40–45 ms. Direct access to multiple Icelandic subsea systems (IRIS, DANICE, FARICE-class). Strong resilience compared to other Arctic sites.
sites.zoning_status: Parcel A fully zoned for DC; Parcel B (expansion) requires further zoning.
sites.current_control: Energy subsidiary (RE) — materially reducing execution risk
sites.area_overview: Reykjavík is the capital and largest city of Iceland. It is located on the southern shore of the Faxaflói bay in southwest Iceland and has a latitude of 64°08′ N, making it the world's northernmost capital of a sovereign state. Reykjavík has a population of around 139,000 as of 2025, and the surrounding Capital Region has a population of around 249,000, constituting approximately 64% of Iceland's population.
sites.hub_distances_km: Frankfurt: 2378 km · Stockholm: 2134 km · London: 1890 km · Amsterdam: 2018 km

sites.name: Kristinestad
sites.desc_en: Large 230ha site in the wind-power heartland of Finland benefiting from outstanding grid capacity.
sites.desc_no: Finsk kystlokasjon i vindkraftens kjerneområde med stor nettkapasitet.
sites.category_rationale: Develop — shovel-ready in ~12 months with ~500 MW of potential consumption. First 60 MW (6-9 mo); next 40 MW (12-24 mo); above 100 MW via 400 kV (24-36 mo).
sites.nbs_assessment: Municipality stated intent to issue a reservation agreement Feb 2026 — after which project can commence in earnest.
sites.project_description: Brought to Scale42 March 2025. Out-bid other developers for a 500 MW+ facility. Strong municipal backing through extensive stakeholder engagement and multiple site visits. Large expanse of dense woodland with undulating topography, situated next to a main highway.
sites.restrictions: No Chinese/Russian end customers/funders. Prefer no Israeli funders or end customers. Do not want APL UK Ltd as part of project.
sites.exact_power_status: Available subject to permits
sites.fibre_general_info: GlobalConnect brief report indicates fibre connectivity sufficient. Telia 400G DWDM proposal: OTC €385k, MRC €6,180/mo.
sites.latency_summary: Frankfurt ~17–31 ms · London ~31–35 ms · NY ~95–105 ms.
sites.airport_access: 1 hr to domestic; 4 hr to international
sites.zoning_status: Reservation agreement out for municipal signature (Feb 2026). Zoning approval targeted Nov/Dec 2026.
sites.key_risks_dependencies: Reservation agreement carries KPIs Scale42 must meet to progress to purchase/leasehold (Q1 2027); KPIs are easily met.
sites.current_control: Reservation agreement out for municipal signature (Feb-11)
sites.contractual_arrangement: DLA Piper Finland; Peter Sladey; Permitting: Ramboll, AINS, Granlund.
sites.plots_secured: We have been told we will be able to secure the land in Sept/Oct 2025
sites.extra_data:
ProjectID: FIN011
Primary Project Partner: Biforst Prioritised 8
Potential FID Date: Land Reservation Agreed - Oct25 Zoning Complete - Nov25 Building Permit Received - May26 Fingrid Power Allocated - May26 FID Date = May26
FID Date Update: Land Reservation Agreed - Dec25 Zoning Complete - Nov25 Building Permit Received - March26 Fingrid Power Allocated - March26 FID Date = March26

FID Risks: Building permit timeline - Can confirm in more detail with Ramboll - Confirm that permit proces can begin before zoning complete - Indicated this can Land reservation delays Another company reserving the power before us (requires building permit and no knowledge of competing projects in process)
Key FID Costs: Land Reservation Signature - 30k EUR (1 year term) Extrernal DD report (elective) - 35k EUR (estimate based upon Ramboll elsewhere) Completion of municipal zoning work - No cost (taken by municipality) Building Application Cost - 60k EUR (estimate based upon Ramboll elsewhere) Fingrid Application Fee (110kV) - 900k-1.8mil EUR or Fingrid Application Fee (400kV) - 2.3mil EUR Land lease fee (After 1 year reservation term) - 1.2mil - 3.6mil per annum (540ha) (should FID process take longer than 1 year from signature of land reservation)
Potential RFS Dates: Assuming 9 month construction commencing May 2026 RFS Phase 1 = December 2026
Project Lead: Thomas Beaton
Agent: Peter Sladey - NMG Estonia
Key Project Contacts: Richard Lahteenmaki / richard.lahteenmaki@krs.fi Anton Ragnas / anton.ragnas@krs.fi Lauri Koskinen / lauri.koskinen@krs.fi Mila Segervall / mila.segervall@krs.fi Pertti Miettunen / pertti.miettunen@krs.fi
Plot Name: Lalby
Plot Priority: 1
Site Address: Kristinestad, Finland 64100
Location Link: https://maps.app.goo.gl/xM4u7x8DMyR7Fw5D9
Not Required: 62.273057, 21.432200
Maps & Drawings: Insert Link
Photos & Drone Footage: Insert Link
Physical Inspection Conducted: No
Size (ha) - Primary Plot: 240
Description: Large expanse of dense woodland with undulating topography, situated next to a main highway.
Site Drainage: The site has potential for great access, with the 663 highway skirting the southern border of the plot. An additional access point could be created to the main road adjacent to the north west of the property. Due to the large expanse of this land plot, there are many stream and hydological considerations to be made before site plans can be made.
Site Access: The site has potential for great access, with the 663 highway skirting the southern border of the plot. An additional access point could be created to the main road adjacent to the north west of the property. Due to the large expanse of this land plot, there are many stream and hydological considerations to be made before site plans can be made.
Building Rights (%) - Primary Plot: TBC (somewhere between 50% - 70%). If 50% rezoning to 70% would be a quick process
Ownership: Kristinestad Municipality
Zoning Status: Zoning ongoing led by the municipality and forecast for completion in November 2025
Heat Off-Take: Small District Heating Network in Place, investigations not yet undertaken
Fibre Connectivity: A brief report has been received from GlobalConnect which indicates Fibre connectivity is sufficient. Please reference report in system and meeting notes with GlobalConnect for more detailed information.
Power Connection Methods: 110kV lines run over the site across the Northern boundary making for an easy connection. It is suggested that 2 x 110kV connections are planned following the existing power corridor north and connecting to the 110kV substation Alternative ability to run a single 400kV line along the same power corridor to the same substation. Substation has an expected capacity of over 1000MW
Power Connection Timelines: 60MW overhead line connections - 6 months 100MW direct connections -18 to 24 months 400kV connection could be subject to different timelines
Power Procurement Status: Once a building permit is approved the power can be immidiately allocated by Fingrid by us submitting a simple application.
Power Availability Confirmation: No, via municipality and agent
Acquisition Strategy: The municipality is finalising internal procedures to allocate the site to Scale42. Thiis would be offered at a low cost of 30,000 EUR. Answers: -100G -Yes, black fiber exists. WDM for wavelength services, and also dedicated leased lines. So it’s a yes for every question. -Latency (ping times) is 5-30ms. The fibre trunk route goes along road number 502 to Polvijärvi, and a fibre cable junction needs to be made from this trunk route. The connection price depends on the distance between the trunk line and the data centre. When the accurate location of the centre is decided, the cost of the underground data cable can be calculated. sites.area_overview: The Kiteezi Landfill, also known as the Kiteezi Rubbish Dump, is a solid waste disposal site in Uganda. It is the main solid waste disposal site for the capital city of Kampala, with a projected metropolitan population of approximately 4 million people, as of 2024, serving the homes, businesses and industries of that metropolis.

sites.name: Sørfold
sites.category_rationale: Dispose — shovel-ready in near term. Power connection 24–36 months per Ramboll report — not a near-term development site.
sites.nbs_assessment: Zoning has faced political challenges due to old master plan, but accelerating since Nov 2025. Majority of municipal political parties voting in favour. Working with administration before detailed regulation of the landplot.
sites.project_description: Identified by Scale42 in 2021; in zoning approval process since identification and land option. Undulating greenfield site in the centre of a valley with a mix of grassland and natural woodland. "50 MW allocated; 50 MW+ expansion potential".
sites.exact_power_status: Allocated and available
sites.fibre_general_info: Served via Nordlandsnett with access to Telenor, Telia and GlobalConnect. Strong for northern Norway; final allocation and route diversity to be locked down. International reach indirect via Sweden/Denmark into DANICE / IRIS — not a cable-landing location.
sites.latency_summary: Oslo ~8–12 ms · Stockholm ~10–15 ms · Frankfurt ~25–30 ms · Amsterdam ~25–30 ms · London ~30–35 ms.
sites.other_access_notes: 5 km to commercial port. Multiple data-centre projects ongoing in area; NATO establishing offices in nearby city.
sites.zoning_status: Zoning being changed from LNF to industry (with data centre) with political support. Working with ABO Plan & Arkitektur on detailed zoning. Permits expected to be approved at or shortly after zoning.
sites.key_risks_dependencies: Re-zoning requires more political capital than other regions; current political will is good.
sites.current_control: Secure option agreement with landowner; full TSO 50 MW allocation

ProjectID: NOR002
Primary Project Partner: Biforst Prioritised 8

Project Lead: Bendik Fostervoll
Agent: No agent
Plot Name: Gjerelvmo
Plot Priority: 1
Site Address: Kobbvassveien 90, 8264 Engan, Norway
Location Link: https://maps.app.goo.gl/LnKqLDBreevj2ZUN6
Not Required: 67.639736, 15.979230
Maps & Drawings: Insert Link
Photos & Drone Footage: Insert Link
Physical Inspection Conducted: Yes
Size (ha) - Primary Plot: 5.5
Description: Undulating greenfield site in the centre of a valley with a mix of grassland and natural woodland
Site Drainage: ~2.5km from the E6 highway, the site has good access and connections to Bodo. The single track road from the main road to the site is currently used for forestry and quarry machinery so would be suitable for construction phases, however this road may need upgrading to account for operational data centre traffic. There are no streams running through the site, however the is a river to the south of the side, where run-off water from the site could be directed.
Site Access: ~2.5km from the E6 highway, the site has good access and connections to Bodo. The single track road from the main road to the site is currently used for forestry and quarry machinery so would be suitable for construction phases, however this road may need upgrading to account for operational data centre traffic. There are no streams running through the site, however the is a river to the south of the side, where run-off water from the site could be directed.
Building Rights (%) - Primary Plot: Plot is not detailed regulated yet, % will be decided during regulation Detailed regulation is the last stage in the zoning process
Ownership: Jan Steinar Johansen (Private)
Zoning Status: In progress with zoning projected to complete 2025
Heat Off-Take: Potential to bring in heat offtakers local to the DC site, or provide heated storage facilities.
Fibre Connectivity: Expecting dark dibre availability and 2+ carriers available at most locations identified in Norway. Otherwise Stelia have confirmed across all Norway sites that sufficient dark fibre connectivity for AI data centres can be implemented. James is continuing investigations directly with local fibre providers to obtain more detailed information.
Power Connection Methods: The landplot is adjoining the Gjerelvmo substation directly to the South of the plot. This substation is fed 132kV and stepped down to 22kV The overhead lines currently running through the landplot from south to north are 22kV and will be buried. We will plan a new 22kV connection connecting to 8xNew breakers at the local substation to supply the required load.
Power Connection Timelines: 132kV line is existing and connected to the local substation. Needs a new main transformer at the local substation. New transformer/gas-insulated switchgear/component timeline is likely 2 years and timeline for concession is likely to be approximately 1 year. Therefore expecting a 3 year timeline but seeking to shorten this through discussions with NVE who give approvals for the concessions to speed up the concession process. Also connecting the DSO with suppliers we know who can supply transformers more quickly. Where those suppliers are not acceptable to the DSO we will engage domestic electrical engineering firms about recertifying and warranting transformers from alternative suppliers to the DSO.
Power Procurement Status: Have a full power allocation for 50MW approved by Statnet
Power Availability Confirmation: Yes
Acquisition Strategy: We have a secured option agreement for the land which remains in effect until all zoning and regulatory work is complete. Upon complete of zoning work we would need to purchase the land which is provisioned for within the option agreement.
Zoning Strategy: The land is currently regulated for LNF and is being rezoned to allow data centre use. We have been previously guided by the municipality that zoning is likely to be completed in Q1 27. This is being undertaken with assistance from ABO Plan & Arkitektur. Due to engagement of new lobbying partners and positive meetings between them and the mayor we expect that the zoning could be resolved in a faster process (possibly up to 12 months faster).
Building Permit Strategy: We have been guided by the municipality that building permits can be submitted early to be approved at the same time as the zoning is completed. We would look to use ABO Plan & Arkitektur or Boarch to prepare and submit building permits prior to construction beginning. Commencing investigations into the time and cost of this. Expect building permits to take a couple of months.
Power Procurement Strategy: 50MW has been secured for the site, with an additional 50MW in the que.
Power Provider: Kystnett AS
Power Connection Costs: Latest estimate received from our advisors was approx 15m USD for a 50MW infrastructure contribution toward a 22kV connection plus our costs involved in the connection.
Fibre Provider: Nord-Salten Kraft AS
Price / sqm: 30 NOK per sqm
Lease Rate (per annum): NA
Purchase Price: 1 650 000 NOK (Dont have to buy the whole plot)
Fees: Lobbying partners: Upon successful completion of the zoning process - 2% SPV shares - 25k USD / MW in fees Payable upon the SPV receiving development capital
Next Steps: Await progress within the zoning process. Political meetings are expected to be held in June to better explain the project to local politicians.

sites.name: Torsboda
sites.bid_status: Lead *hot* (was Project, changed by TB & WT 19/02)
sites.category_rationale: Close / Dispose — shovel-ready 2028 with ~400 MW potential. Power connection 2029-2030 so RFS much longer. Strategic location with proximity to multiple hydropower projects.
sites.nbs_assessment: Bid submitted Jan 2026 against several other DC companies. Result expected Feb 2026. Early feedback indicated front-runner status.
sites.project_description: Brought to Scale42 January 2026 by Peter Sladey. Zoned land near Sundsvall, SE2 Sweden, with ~1,200 MW of power and an electrification timeline. ~15% slope on the westward side of a hill; managed woodland cleared for industrial use.
sites.exact_power_status: Power becomes available and allocated to site owner in 2028.
sites.fibre_general_info: Strategic central-eastern Sweden location with dense carrier presence and short routes to continental Europe. Strong for latency-sensitive workloads.
sites.latency_summary: Stockholm ~5–8 ms · Frankfurt ~18–22 ms · Amsterdam ~18–22 ms · London ~22–25 ms.
sites.zoning_status: Already zoned (190 ha, 30% building rights). Power connection target 2027.
sites.key_risks_dependencies: Currently in competition with other bidders; Scale42 has strong municipal track record.

sites.desc_en: North Ostrobothnia site with strong onshore wind and existing 110 kV connection.
sites.desc_no: Lokasjon i Nord-Österbotten med sterk landvind og eksisterende 110 kV-tilknytning.
sites.category_rationale: Develop* or Dispose — shovel-ready in near term.
sites.nbs_assessment: Reservation negotiations with municipality. Scale42 LOI submitted to secure negotiations. Backing for removal of nearby road-bound airstrip.
sites.project_description: Identified by Scale42 October 2025. LOI submitted Jan 2026 for plot near a 400 kV substation; potential to grow to >500 MW. Flat land with slight undulation, mostly managed woodland.
sites.exact_power_status: Available subject to permits
sites.fibre_general_info: Within Finland's national fibre network; site-specific connectivity TBC. Regional utility fibre with access to long-haul routes south.
sites.latency_summary: Frankfurt ~17–31 ms · London ~31–35 ms · NY ~95–105 ms.
sites.zoning_status: Outline zoning required. Industrial zoning (with DC permission) expected by Q4 2026. Sweco commissioned for environmental study.

sites.restrictions: No Chinese/Russian end customers / funders. No crypto.

sites.fibre_general_info: Telia 400G DWDM proposal: OTC €395k, MRC €6,780/mo.

sites.zoning_status: Detailed planning expected to finish summer 2026.

sites.name: Robertsfors
sites.category_rationale: Dispose / Close / Mining — shovel-ready in 24 months, >800 MW potential. Near northern Finnish (sic — Swedish) power-house, strategically located for long-term expansion.
sites.nbs_assessment: Project introduced by JLL into GIGA-42; could present a good project in future.
sites.project_description: Brought to GIGA-42 by JLL. Sale of land by Holmen. Viewed as a follow-on for other Swedish projects re: time to powered land. Land near a main highway outside Robertsfors with a mix of open grassland and forestry.
sites.exact_power_status: Phased delivery starting 2029 via Skellefteå Kraft.
sites.fibre_general_info: Strong regional fibre with access to Sweden's national backbone; suitable for scalable AI and cloud deployments.
sites.latency_summary: Stockholm ~10–12 ms · Frankfurt ~22–26 ms · Amsterdam ~22–26 ms · London ~26–30 ms.
sites.hub_distances_km: Frankfurt: 1723 km · Stockholm: 561 km · London: 1862 km · Amsterdam: 1603 km

sites.name: Nuuk
sites.category_rationale: Develop / Dispose — long lead, but strategic: (i) placement between Europe, NA & Pacific; (ii) commercial foothold in Greenland / expanded company footprint; (iii) media coverage for development.
sites.nbs_assessment: Early-stage discussions with municipality keen to locate a DC near Nuuk airport. Municipality very keen to involve Scale42 due to Glomfjord precedent. Hydropower expansion planned to increase availability.
sites.project_description: Brought to GIGA-42 JV for exploration of building a DC near Nuuk airport, Greenland. Greenland government desiring to move away from North American businesses. Verbal confirmation from senior NunaGreen business developer that GIGA-42 are preferred partners (Glomfjord track record cited). Exposed rock and undulated topography adjacent to Nuuk airport.
sites.exact_power_status: Not verified
sites.fibre_general_info: Nuuk's fibre is a limited but strategic subsea footprint with national coverage and international access via Iceland. Effectively single-region from a fibre perspective. Best suited to sovereign compute, research, government, edge or specialised workloads where jurisdiction/climate/energy matter more than ultra-low latency.
sites.latency_summary: Reykjavik ~15–20 ms · Copenhagen ~35–45 ms · London ~45–55 ms · Frankfurt ~50–60 ms · NY ~40–50 ms. Resilience depends on Greenland–Iceland corridor and downstream routing.
sites.zoning_status: Status of planning, zoning and permits TBC; not perceived as risk due to long power timeline.
sites.key_risks_dependencies: Although low, geo-political risks to Greenland projects.
sites.area_overview: Nuuk is the capital and most populous city of Greenland, an autonomous territory of Denmark. It is the seat of the Sermersooq municipality and the government of Greenland and is the territory's largest cultural and economic center. In January 2025, it had a population of 20,113—more than a third of the territory's population—making it one of the smallest capital cities in the world by population.
sites.hub_distances_km: Frankfurt: 3784 km · Stockholm: 3521 km · London: 3245 km · Amsterdam: 3420 km

sites.name: Tjeldbergodden
sites.category_rationale: Develop or Mining or Close — does not meet 18-month RFS criterion (more like 30 months). Existing heat-offtaker (DC heat for adjacent fish farm). Strong ESG/media angle. Potential mining play if heat off-take enables substantial power savings (Eltax exemption).
sites.nbs_assessment: Early-stage discussions with major planned aquaculture development. Scale42 would take surplus power and land for DC; waste heat to fish farm. Formal agreements still to complete.
sites.project_description: Early-stage discussions with Salfjord AS, a local aquaculture business exploring waste heat coupling for salmon farming. Located next to the sea half-way up the Norwegian coast, sloping topography on exposed rocky base.
sites.restrictions: Must provision heat supply to neighbouring aquaculture producer "Salfjord" as a project condition.
sites.exact_power_status: Power application submitted via project partner
sites.fibre_strands: Dark fibre available, landing point next to site
sites.fibre_general_info: Tjeldbergodden is served by Norway's national carrier ecosystem with high-capacity services available. Proximity to industrial infrastructure and transport corridors provides strong ducting and last-mile options. Final carrier selection and physical path diversity TBC.
sites.latency_summary: Oslo ~5–10 ms · Stockholm ~10–18 ms · Copenhagen ~12–20 ms · Frankfurt ~20–30 ms · Amsterdam ~20–30 ms · London ~25–35 ms. Indirect via Norwegian/Danish/UK subsea systems with strong European breakout.
sites.airport_access: 1.2 hr to domestic (KSU); 2 hr to domestic w/ international connections (TRD)
sites.college_university: 1.2 hr to college; 2 hr to university
sites.zoning_status: Detailed zoning plan submitted to Aure Municipality for 142.5 daa across gnr. 51, bnr. 1/6/44 (industry / data centre). Planning expected to complete early 2027.
sites.key_risks_dependencies: Competition emerging via on-going negotiations with Salfjord AS; Scale42 has strong track-record of out-competing other DC entities.
sites.hub_distances_km: Frankfurt: 1480 km · Stockholm: 679 km · London: 1420 km · Amsterdam: 1248 km

sites.name: Honningsvåg
sites.category_rationale: Mining or Dispose — small (15 MW) far-north Norway. Best mining location in the Nordics: no electricity tax, lowest power price in Europe (avg 8.71 EUR/MWh in 2025), no employment tax, municipality and landowners open to any form of employment incl. mining.
sites.nbs_assessment: Secure but small (15 MW) far north of Norway. Power 8.71 EUR/MWh in 2025 incl. taxes. Lower employment tax in municipality.
sites.project_description: Identified by Scale42 in 2023; potentially the second-most-northerly data centre globally (after Prudhoe Bay, Alaska). Rough rocky arctic terrain on the outskirts of Honningsvåg near the airport.
sites.exact_power_status: In queue, awaiting allocation. 1st in local DSO queue.
sites.heat_reuse: Municipal support for study engaging local businesses.
sites.fibre_general_info: Local fibre served via regional utility (Area Nett); specific named carrier beyond utility not confirmed.
sites.latency_summary: Oslo ~15–20 ms · Stockholm ~20–25 ms · Frankfurt ~30–35 ms · London ~35–40 ms. Subsea: indirect via southern Norway / Sweden.
sites.college_university: 15 min to college; domestic flight to university
sites.key_risks_dependencies: Workforce — population ~3,000, but Scale42 has trained data-centre teams in similarly small communities (Glomfjord, pop ~2,000, 30 MW).

James is continuing investigations directly with local fibre providers to obtain more detailed information.
Power Connection Methods: There is approximately 5,500 kVA of available capacity in the existing 22kV grid feeding the land plot. For any further increase in capacity beyond this, it will be necessary to replace the main transformers at the Storbukt substation. However, space within the existing building is limited, and cannot accommodate significantly larger transformers. As a result, structural expansion of the facility would be required. Additionally, the existing 66 kV lines limit the total allowable load, and the maximum feasible transformer size is estimated at approximately 2 x 30 MVA. Upgrades to the distribution network between the Storbukt substation and the project site will also be necessary.

sites.area_overview: Honningsvåg is the northernmost city/town in mainland Norway. It is located in Nordkapp Municipality in Finnmark county. Honningsvåg was declared a city in 1996, despite its small population. The 1.04-square-kilometre (260-acre) town has a population of 2,245 (2023), which gives the town a population density of 2,159 inhabitants per square kilometre (5,590/sq mi).

sites.name: Kemi
sites.category_rationale: Develop / Close / Mining — near-term shovel-ready (~45 MW) with 1000+ MW long-term. Complex brownfield. Acquisition needs to be attractive. 70 MW currently allocated to a bitcoin miner; if unlocked, ~115 MW near-term.
sites.nbs_assessment: Advanced acquisition stage. One of a small number of buyers invited to third stage of buyer selection / DD. Project securable with capital. Possible +70 MW starting capacity (subject to negotiations).
sites.project_description: Identified by Peter Sladey March 2025. Brownfield industrial island with own power infrastructure, potential to draw ~1 GW (with upgrades). Existing tenants and heavy-set industrial buildings. Two points of entry to the island. Flat industrial island in northern Finland.
sites.exact_power_status: Available subject to permits
sites.fibre_general_info: Existing fibre sufficient for data centre; needs build to site (~1.5 km east). Possible private network in old paper mill area. Telia 400G DWDM proposal: OTC €255k, MRC €8,020/mo.
sites.latency_summary: Frankfurt ~17–31 ms · London ~31–35 ms · NY ~95–105 ms.
sites.airport_access: 10 km to domestic; 2 hr to international
sites.zoning_status: Already zoned; 190 ha site with 30% building rights.
sites.key_risks_dependencies: Currently in competition with other bidders for the industrial site; Scale42 has municipal backing.

Plot Name: Veitsiluoto
Plot Priority: 1
Site Address: Päätie 50, 94800 Kemi, Finland
Location Link: https://maps.app.goo.gl/5uy7R2vMHRn2Z3hJ7
Not Required: 65.692448, 24.622775
Maps & Drawings: Insert Link
Photos & Drone Footage: Insert Link
Physical Inspection Conducted: No
Size (ha) - Primary Plot: 190
Description: Flat industrial island sheltered by peninsulas either side, located in the north of Finland
Site Drainage: The existing brownfield site has perfect access via main roads at either end of the island, leaving little in terms of required infrastructure upgrades. Being flat and adjacent to water bodies, the site has no issue with drainage.
Site Access: The existing brownfield site has perfect access via main roads at either end of the island, leaving little in terms of required infrastructure upgrades. Being flat and adjacent to water bodies, the site has no issue with drainage.
Ownership: Stora Enso Veitsiluoto Oy
Zoning Status: Zoned
Heat Off-Take: Nearby district heating network and adjacent existing industrial buildings would likely take waste heat from the facility.
Fibre Connectivity: Existing fibre connectivity today sufficient to support data centre (ref existing development) and the existing industries. Otherwise investigations continue. Globalconnect has a route around Finland close to this location
Power Connection Methods: Existing main grid connection at Fingrid’s Isohaara switch yard (2 x 110 kV) One of these connections is reserved and the other can support up to 310MW. much of this capacity is already reserved, clarifying what is available on this line. maybe 50-60MW Phase 1 would utilise the existing available 110kV connection at the site. Phase 2 would require an application to connect a new 400kV cable 20km to the Simojoki substation which is likely to take 3-4 years and could supply an additional 600MW. See detailed report from Ramboll for further information - if building a new 110kV line would be connecting to a much closer substation
Power Connection Timelines: Existing 110kV connection would be near immidiate. An additional 400kV connection to substation is estimated at 3-4 years.
Power Procurement Status: Once a building permit is approved the power can be immidiately allocated by Fingrid by us submitting a simple application.
Power Availability Confirmation: No, via municipality and agent
Acquisition Strategy: Receive all information about the Island and industrial park Identify preffered plot in Industrial park for data centre development Undertake detailed DD Negotiate with StorEnso an agreement to purchase the island and industrial park Otherwise negotiate to acquire the desired land plot in isolation for DC development.
Zoning Strategy: Site has correct zoning
Building Permit Strategy: A technical consultant such as Ramboll can be instructed to prepare and submit the building permits. Including technical design this is forecast to cost circa 60k EUR. The strategy would be to submit short form building permits which can be prepared, submitted and approved in 2-4 months.
Power Procurement Strategy: Once a short-form building permit is approved, an application can be made to Fingrid including ramp up plans and connection details. This would then be instantly approved and capacity allocated for a new 400kV line. The existing line 110kV line would not require a connection application.
Power Provider: FinGrid
Power Connection Costs: 2.3mil EUR per 400kV connection plus cost of construction. No cost expected to use the existing 110kV connection.
Fibre Provider: Global Connect likely best positioned to provide
Fees: Agents fees: 10% of 1 year rent or 2% of land purchase price (on signing of land contract) plus 2,500 EUR / MW (on signing of power contract)
Next Steps: Agree price for site with Stora Enso.

sites.area_overview: Kemi is a town and municipality of Finland. It is located approximately 30 kilometres (19 mi) from the city of Tornio and the Swedish border. The distance to Oulu is 105 kilometres (65 mi) to the south and to Rovaniemi is 117 kilometres (73 mi) to the northeast. It was founded in 1869 by a decree of the Emperor Alexander II of Russia because of its proximity to a deepwater port.

sites.name: Mikkeli
sites.category_rationale: Develop — landplot secured by reservation. Ready to commence permits. Achieving max power likely requires capital or off-take commitment to DSO; otherwise capacity capped ~50 MW.
sites.nbs_assessment: Outline zoning in place; progressing detailed regulation. Permits can run in parallel. DSO can install new substation breakers once permits are in. "Good site in my opinion."
sites.project_description: Brought to Scale42 March 2025 to develop a data centre just outside Mikkeli, a regional and rapidly expanding hub in central Finland. Heavy engagement with both the municipality and the local DSO. Flat arable/forestry mix with ~2 m topographic differences.
sites.exact_power_status: Available subject to permits & DSO agreement
sites.fibre_general_info: Telia 400G DWDM: insufficient DWDM capacity for this connection currently.
sites.latency_summary: Frankfurt ~17–31 ms · London ~31–35 ms · NY ~95–105 ms.
sites.airport_access: 10 km to domestic; 2 hr to international
sites.zoning_status: Reservation agreement signed. Zoning approval target Nov/Dec 2026.
sites.key_risks_dependencies: Two additional data-centre operators planning in Mikkeli — possible competition for power and labour. Project may be delayed by DSO grid-works approvals.

The existing access to the South should be improved and used as an emergency access route. Debtate over who should pay for the Western road connection, us or municipality. Approximate cost is 1mil EUR / km and the distance would be 500m. There is currently drainage which collects stormwater North of the site and then runs through a channel toward the Western boundary of the site from North to South. This would need addressing.
Site Access: Site is accessed currently from small roads to the North East & South. The access to the North East would need to be improved and new access implemented from the West over Natural Resources Finland land. The municipality will arrange this. The existing access to the South should be improved and used as an emergency access route. Debtate over who should pay for the Western road connection, us or municipality. Approximate cost is 1mil EUR / km and the distance would be 500m. There is currently drainage which collects stormwater North of the site and then runs through a channel toward the Western boundary of the site from North to South. This would need addressing.
Building Rights (%) - Primary Plot: TBC (somewhere between 50% - 70%). If 50% rezoning to 70% would be a quick process
Ownership: 20ha - Mikkeli Municipality 6ha - Natural Resources Finland (State Owned)
Zoning Status: Ongoing by municipality - Expected early 2026, or late 2025 if undertaken privately.
Heat Off-Take: District Heating under investigation or direct supply of heat to commercial land being zoned and developed adjacent to the Karilla land plot. Municipality have offered to open negotiations with local district heating company (ESE) which is majority municipality owned. An LOI from ESE for heat use would improve public support for the project.
Fibre Connectivity: Initial discussion with local Fibre provider (MPY) "Site #2 seems to be built on a lot that currently already holds access to MPY's network. These assessments should be considered as initial and visual interpretations, which will be further defined ones more details on the sites are provided." "The theoretical maximum bandwith is 1Tb (built with 10x100G connections)." See email 26th February 2025
Power Connection Methods: There are currently 110kV overhead lines running through the middle of the landplot. These are to be moved to run along the Eastern Boundary of the site in a new "power corridor". Ramboll is conducting a construction feasibility study for this work which will be available in August. Fingrid have told the municipality that there is up to 250MW of capacity on the 110kV lines. Question whether Fingrid would allocate more than100MW for a single connection on 110kV lines. Option 1: Direct overhead connection to the 110kV lines which will be adjacent to the site Option 2: Connect to nearby 110kV substation (750m to North) - ESE Verkko (50MW to 100MW) Option 3: Construct 1 or 2 x 110kV lines from site to Visulahti substation from the site along the existing powercorridor and overhead lines. Each supporting max 250MW. Visuhlati substation has over 1000MW available with planned upgrades in the next 24 months
Power Connection Timelines: 60MW overhead line connections - 6 months 100MW direct connections -18 to 24 months
Power Procurement Status: Once a building permit is approved the power can be immidiately allocated by Fingrid by us submitting a simple application.
Power Availability Confirmation: No, via municipality and agent
Acquisition Strategy: Option 1: Do Nothing - Land will go to public auction in 2026 and we could participate in the public process or use the backup land plot identified. Option 2: Make a proposal to the municipality to reserve the land for us today. This proposal should include: - That we continue the zoning process privately. - That the purchase price would be 11.6 EUR - 23 EUR per square metre - The site can be rented for up to 30 years at 7.5% of the purchase price with right to purchase at any time - Consider whether we wish to pay for the Western Access Road - Who would be our zoning consultant - Who would be our legal counsel - What kind of data centre - Who will be the owners - What activities will take place at the data centre - How much employment will be created - Any other considerations We should engage a zoning consultant prior to the proposal to understand the zoning cost and for additional guidance on the land price proposal. One has been identified The proposal would then be reviwewed by the city board and then if improved is reffered to the city council for final decision. Once reaching city council this becomes a political decision and public process.
Zoning Strategy: It is suggested to engage a zoning consultant. A well-reputed zoning consultant with good ties to the local municipality has been identified. "Austa Consulting". We will be reaching out to them shortly to discuss a scope of work and fees.
Building Permit Strategy: A technical consultant such as Ramboll can be instructed to prepare and submit the building permits. Including technical design this is forecast to cost circa 60k EUR. The strategy would be to submit short form building permits which can be prepared, submitted and approved in 2-4 months.
Power Procurement Strategy: Once a short-form building permit is approved, an application can be made to Fingrid including ramp up plans and connection details. This would then be instantly approved and capacity allocated.
Power Provider: FinGrid / ESE Verkko
Power Connection Costs: 900k EUR per 110kV connection plus the cost of construction.
Fibre Provider: MPY
Price / sqm: Expected in the range of 23 EUR psm subject to proposal from us
Lease Rate (per annum): 7.5% of purchase price annually - €412,500 pa
Purchase Price: €5,500,000.00
Fees: Agents fees: 10% of 1 year rent or 2% of land purchase price (on signing of land contract) plus 2,500 EUR / MW (on signing of power contract)
Next Steps: Engage zoning consultant Submit proposal to municipality

sites.area_overview: Mikkeli is a city in, and the regional capital of, South Savo, Finland, located in the Finnish Lakeland. The population is approximately 52,000, while the Mikkeli sub-region of South Savo has a population of approximately 68,000. Mikkeli is the 18th most-populous municipality of Finland and the 19th most-populous urban area in the country.

sites.area_overview: Boden is a locality and the seat of Boden Municipality in Norrbotten County, Sweden, with 16,847 inhabitants in 2018. It is part of the larger area around coastal city Luleå some 36 kilometres (22 mi) southeast. After Kiruna, it is the second largest town in Northern Sweden's interior.

sites.area_overview: Halse og Harkmark is a former municipality in the old Vest-Agder county, Norway. The 105-square-kilometre (41 sq mi) municipality existed from 1838 until its dissolution in 1964. The administrative centre of the municipality was the village of Halse, which at that time was a suburb of the town of Mandal. It was located in the southern part of the municipality of Mandal. The municipality of Halse og Harkmark encompassed the rural areas that surrounded the town of Mandal, including many islands such as Hille, Skjernøy, and Pysen. It is now located within Lindesnes Municipality in what is now Agder county.

sites.area_overview: (Norwegian) or Suortá (Northern Sami) is a municipality in Nordland county, Norway. It is part of the traditional region of Vesterålen. The administrative centre of the municipality is the town of Sortland. Other population centres in Sortland include the villages of Bø, Holand, Holmstad, Liland, Sigerfjord, Strand, and Vik. The Norwegian Coast Guard has its northern base in Sortland, called Kystvaktskvadron Nord.

sites.name: Salsbruket
sites.category_rationale: Mining or Close — smaller opportunity from a hydropower producer. Land adjacent to power plant and a fish farm requiring heat. Not progressed due to small capacity (5 MW) but remains as a lead.
sites.nbs_assessment: Smaller opportunity from hydropower producer. Land adjacent to power plant and fish farm requiring heat. Remains a lead.
sites.project_description: Behind-the-meter power supply from a hydropower plant owned by Albert Collett. Potential delivery of waste heat to a local MOWI fish farm. No power application required for behind-the-meter supply. Additional power potentially via existing 66 kV / 22 kV substation. Good water access for cooling. Proof-of-concept before access to Collett's wider hydro portfolio.
sites.exact_power_status: Available subject to agreement with landowner who is also the utility
sites.fibre_general_info: Central Norway location with access to national fibre routes; final connectivity dependent on confirmed site selection.
sites.latency_summary: Oslo ~8–12 ms · Frankfurt ~25–30 ms · London ~30–35 ms.
sites.airport_access: 1 hr to domestic; 4 hr to domestic w/ international connections (TRD); two airports in between
sites.college_university: 1 hr to university; 2.5 hr to university

Project Lead: Daniel Gussias
Agent: No agent
Plot Priority: 1
Maps & Drawings: Insert Link
Photos & Drone Footage: Insert Link
Fibre Connectivity: Expecting dark dibre availability and 2+ carriers available at most locations identified in Norway. Otherwise Stelia have confirmed across all Norway sites that sufficient dark fibre connectivity for AI data centres can be implemented. James is continuing investigations directly with local fibre providers to obtain more detailed information.
Power Connection Methods: There are currently 110kV overhead lines running through the middle of the landplot. These are to be moved to run along the Eastern Boundary of the site in a new "power corridor". Ramboll is conducting a construction feasibility study for this work which will be available in August. Fingrid have told the municipality that there is up to 250MW of capacity on the 110kV lines. Question whether Fingrid would allocate more than100MW for a single connection on 110kV lines. Option 1: Direct overhead connection to the 110kV lines which will be adjacent to the site Option 2: Connect to nearby 110kV substation (750m to North) - ESE Verkko (50MW to 100MW) Option 3: Construct 1 or 2 x 110kV lines from site to Visulahti substation from the site along the existing powercorridor and overhead lines. Each supporting max 250MW. Visuhlati substation has over 1000MW available with planned upgrades in the next 24 months
Power Connection Timelines: We understand that no permits are required by Fingrid for this form of direct connection which should enable a fast process. Investigations ongoing as to whether additional bays would need to be constructed at the substation.
Power Procurement Status: Seeking direct supply agreement with hydropower plant owner. Discussions ongoing
Power Availability Confirmation: No - confirmed by local hydropower plant owner.
Acquisition Strategy: We are currently negotiating the terms of an option agreement and subsequent lease with option to purchase.
Fees: None

sites.name: Rovaniemi
sites.category_rationale: Dispose / Close / Mining — shovel-ready in 24 months, >800 MW potential. Near central Finnish power corridor; strategic for long-term expansion.
sites.nbs_assessment: Early-stage; site explored June 2025 then halted, awaiting bandwidth to chase again with hydropower operator Kemijoki Oy.
sites.project_description: Identified April 2025 near the Pirtikoski hydrodam alongside Business Rovaniemi. Discussions halted in expectation of finding a more workforce-friendly location. Gently sloping site with sporadic woodland and multiple access roads, adjacent to 400 kV substation and hydropower dam.
sites.exact_power_status: Available subject to permits
sites.fibre_general_info: Rovaniemi sits on Finland's national backbone with Elisa and Telia present, dark fibre available subject to strand allocation. Routes south toward Helsinki for international subsea breakout. Site-specific routing and commercial terms to be validated upon reactivation.
sites.latency_summary: Frankfurt ~17–31 ms · London ~31–35 ms · NY ~95–105 ms.

ProjectID: FIN012
Potential FID Date: Zoning Complete - Nov26 Building Permit Received - May27 Fingrid Power Allocated - May27 FID Date = May27
FID Date Update: Is Zoning ongoing or yet to commence? Is a Nov26 zoning timeline achievable? Relies upon clean transition permit.

Agent: Peter Sladey - NMG Estonia

Plot Name: "Pirttikoski 1"
Plot Priority: 1
Site Address: Voimalaitostie 153, 97635 Rovaniemi, Finland
Location Link: https://maps.app.goo.gl/HQEmmXhQ2sum14YP6
Maps & Drawings: Insert Link
Photos & Drone Footage: Insert Link
Physical Inspection Conducted: No
Size (ha) - Primary Plot: 55
Description: Gently sloping site with sporadic woodland and multiple access roads running through it. Adjacent to 400kv substation & hydropower dam.
Site Drainage: The currently selected site has multiple access points, however all are single track (tarmac). Nearest main road is ~600m from the site, so investment into the local infrastructure could be necassary. With a gentle slope in the direction of the nearby gorge, drainage/ run-off will not be an issue on site.
Site Access: The currently selected site has multiple access points, however all are single track (tarmac). Nearest main road is ~600m from the site, so investment into the local infrastructure could be necassary. With a gentle slope in the direction of the nearby gorge, drainage/ run-off will not be an issue on site.
Building Rights (%) - Primary Plot: Estimated 70% - We can request this in zoning process
Ownership: Hydropower Plant Operator
Zoning Status: Not zoned for DC and likley to take 18-24 months. Possibility some of the land is already zoned, which could be sufficient and currently investigating.
Heat Off-Take: None
Fibre Connectivity: Fibre should not be an issue but investigations continue. Fingrid have stated that access to existing dark fibre connection at their substation should not be a problem. Also the existing Hydropower plant requires resiliant data connectivity for remote operations.
Power Connection Methods: Direct 400kV or 110kV connection to substation adjoining the land plot and neighbouring hydropower plant.
Power Connection Timelines: We understand that no permits are required by Fingrid for this form of direct connection which should enable a fast process. Investigations ongoing as to whether additional bays would need to be constructed at the substation.
Power Procurement Status: Once a building permit is approved the power can be immidiately allocated by Fingrid by us submitting a simple application.
Power Availability Confirmation: Verbally by local Hydropower plant operator
Acquisition Strategy: Direct agreement is required between Scale42 and the hydropower plant operator. We are currently answering their questions to satisfy any concerns that a DC could pose risk to the nearby hydropower plant operations. Once satisfied we would seek to agree a purchase and lease price for the land. Then aim to agree a lease/option with a rent-free period to cover zoning time. Followed by a paid lease period with options to purchase the land at any time during the lease.
Zoning Strategy: We would seek to engage a zoning consultant familiar with working on commercial projects in this municipality. We would look to confirm the zoning costs and timeline prior to instructing that work to proceed.
Building Permit Strategy: A technical consultant such as Ramboll can be instructed to prepare and submit the building permits. Including technical design this is forecast to cost circa 60k EUR. The strategy would be to submit short form building permits which can be prepared, submitted and approved in 2-4 months.
Power Procurement Strategy: Once a short-form building permit is approved, an application can be made to Fingrid including ramp up plans and connection details. This would then be instantly approved and capacity allocated.
Power Provider: FinGrid
Power Connection Costs: 2.3mil EUR per 400kV connection plus cost of construction.
Price / sqm: Subject to negotiation
Lease Rate (per annum): Subject to negotiation
Purchase Price: Subject to negotiation
Fees: Agents fees: 10% of 1 year rent or 2% of land purchase price (on signing of land contract) plus 2,500 EUR / MW (on signing of power contract)
Next Steps: Continue to answer questions of the landowner before negotiating commercial terms.
sites.area_overview: Rovaniemi is a city in Finland and the regional capital of Lapland. It is located near the Arctic Circle in the northern interior of the country. The population of Rovaniemi is approximately 66,000, while the sub-region has a population of approximately 70,000. It is the 17th most populous municipality in Finland, and the 11th most populous urban area in the country.

Maps & Drawings: Insert Link
Photos & Drone Footage: Insert Link
Physical Inspection Conducted: Yes
Size (ha) - Primary Plot: 28
Description: Relatively flat greenfield plot with slight elevations to Northern side. Previously forested but recently trees have all been removed by the city with some subsequent regrowth.
Site Drainage: The existing brownfield site has access via road to the south of the landplot where utilities are currnetly supplied. A further access road is being developed to the North of the plot. Being flat and adjacent to water bodies, the site has no issue with drainage.
Site Access: The existing brownfield site has access via road to the south of the landplot where utilities are currnetly supplied. A further access road is being developed to the North of the plot. Being flat and adjacent to water bodies, the site has no issue with drainage.
Building Rights (%) - Primary Plot: 70
Ownership: City of Varkaus
Zoning Status: Zoned
Heat Off-Take: Local district heating company interested to acquire heat with district heating connections adjacent to the site.
Fibre Connectivity: Ranta-Luttila DC is in well positioned site from network connectivity perspective ● Site would need more investments on the fiber infrastructure, but building the infrastructure should be relatively easy ● Varkaus DC area has 3 diverse routes to the area: ○ two by Fingrid OPGW (optical fibre composite overhead ground wires), Dark fiber possibility ○ one by Telia terrestrial cable ○ one via VT5 ● DNA and Elisa fiber infrastructures are not that close so additional investments would be required to get their networks connected to Ranta-Luttila ● There are no network core PoPs close to Varkaus DC so the operators would need to invest to DWDM and MPLS equipment at the site ● During discussions with the operators they indicated their willingness to invest in the fiber and equipment infra to the planned DC area See additional info provided by MPY
Power Connection Methods: Existing 110kV substation located 750m to the south of the site with the existing 110kV lines running adjacent to the site. Fingrid have confirmed capacity is available for a 100MW connection. This would begin with a temporary overhead connection ready in approximately 6 months and supporting 60MW. This would be replaced by a later underground cable connection directly to the substation supporting the full 100MW load.
Power Connection Timelines: 60MW overhead line connections - 6 months 100MW direct connections -18 to 24 months
Power Procurement Status: Once a building permit is approved the power can be immidiately allocated by Fingrid by us submitting a simple application.
Power Availability Confirmation: Yes
Acquisition Strategy: Land already acquired by project partner under a reservation agreement.
Zoning Strategy: Site has correct zoning
Building Permit Strategy: Technical consultant Ramboll has been instructed to prepare and submit the building permits. Including technical design at a cost of 60k EUR. Short form building permits are being submitted at the end of June with approval expected by municipality end of September 2025.
Power Procurement Strategy: Once a short-form building permit is approved, an application can be made to Fingrid including ramp up plans and connection details. This would then be instantly approved and capacity allocated.
Power Provider: FinGrid
Power Connection Costs: 900k EUR per 110kV connection plus the cost of construction.
Fibre Provider: MPY Telia VT5 DNA Elisa Fiber Highway Finland Oy GlobalConnect Suomen Erillisverkot Valoo
Price / sqm: 5 EUR per square metre
Lease Rate (per annum): 105,000 EUR per annum
Purchase Price: 1.4mil EUR
Fees: Agents fees: 10% of 1 year rent or 2% of land purchase price (on signing of land contract) plus 2,500 EUR / MW (on signing of power contract)
Next Steps: Await submission and approval of short form building permit in September 2025.

sites.area_overview: Oulu is a major port city in Finland and the regional capital of North Ostrobothnia. It is located on the north-western coast of the country at the mouth of the River Oulu. The population of Oulu is approximately 217,000, while the sub-region has a population of approximately 265,000. It is the 5th most populous municipality in Finland, and the fourth most populous urban area in the country. Oulu is also the most populous city in Northern Finland.
